summary: DHH celebrates NetNewsWire adding a combined view for RSS feeds, a feature Radio UserLand has had since the beginning. He argues that viewing RSS feeds as one continuous flow is superior to clicking through individual items. He finds it puzzling that other RSS readers were slow to adopt this approach. The post ends with DHH noting that the feature is only in beta for paying customers, which prompted him to purchase the software.
key_points: Combined/continuous view for RSS feeds is a faster and more enjoyable way to scan content
Radio UserLand pioneered this approach but other readers were slow to follow
The old item-by-item clicking approach is compared unfavorably to the repetitive clicking in Diablo
DHH was willing to pay for NetNewsWire specifically to access this beta feature
money_quotes: The old-way is reminiscent of the click-fest that is the game Diablo.
It's a much faster and more enjoyable way to quickly scan a bunch of content.
Betas are only for paying customers. Hence, I'm now a paying customer.
key_insight: A continuous, combined view for reading RSS feeds is clearly superior to clicking through items individually, and good UX innovations deserve to be adopted faster.
